title: Market Brief — Tuesday, June 16, 2026
summary: GPU compute is loose across every class. Util-avail stands at 34% for A100, 33% for H100, 41% for H200, and 42% for B200, all comfortably above the oversupply threshold direction after 30-day utilization cratered 18 to 31 percentage points. H200 volatility at 187% annualized is the loudest price signal, screaming that demand discovery is still chaotic on the newest silicon.
paragraphs: GPU compute is loose across every class. Util-avail stands at 34% for A100, 33% for H100, 41% for H200, and 42% for B200, all comfortably above the oversupply threshold direction after 30-day utilization cratered 18 to 31 percentage points. H200 volatility at 187% annualized is the loudest price signal, screaming that demand discovery is still chaotic on the newest silicon.

B200 is the only GPU where inference on Llama 70B turns a profit at medium-tier pricing, and even then barely, losing 7% margin. H100 operators selling at medium rates lose $4.02 per million tokens against a TCO-adjusted cost of $8.78/M. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output is burning cash against any hosted hardware. Provider grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Neutral, Google Strong, DeepSeek Burning Cash, open-source Burning Cash.

H200 spot at $3.35/hr with 41% availability and near-double annualized volatility is overvalued relative to B200 at $4.38/hr, which delivers 53% lower inference cost per token. Buy B200 spot for Llama-class workloads, sell H200 exposure and wait for the utilization floor.
bullets: 42% of B200 compute capacity sits idle by Ornn utilization, the loosest tier in the stack.
33% util-avail on H100 marks an 18.5 percentage point drop in just 30 days.
$0.18 per million output tokens on OpenAI gpt-oss-120b is the cheapest frontier-adjacent price ever tracked.
21.78 on the LLMflation index means top-tier token prices have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$8.78/M TCO-adjusted H100 inference cost makes medium-tier Llama 70B serving a $4.02/M loss per request.
186.8% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI is triple the B200 reading of 61.4%.
